title: Blood Connect – Emergency Blood Donor-Receiver Platform

requirements.text: Implement a user registration system where users sign up as either a Donor or Receiver. Collect: full name, blood group (A+, A-, B+, B-, O+, O-, AB+, AB-), phone number, city/location, age. Include an optional field for last donation date. Store all user profiles in the backend.

quotes: User can sign up as Donor or Receiver
Collect: Name, Blood Group, Phone Number, Location (City), Age

acceptanceCriteria: User can choose Donor or Receiver role during registration
All required fields (name, blood group, phone, city, age) are validated before submission
Last donation date field is optional and skippable
Registered user profiles are persisted in the Motoko backend
Duplicate phone numbers are rejected with an error message

requirements.text: Build a Smart Donor Search screen where a user enters a required blood group and city. The backend returns matching donors sorted by last active time. Display an 'Available Now' badge on donors who registered or updated their profile within the last 24 hours. Show each donor's name, blood group, city, and phone number in the results list.

quotes: User enters required blood group and location
AI should show nearest available donors
Sort by distance and last active time

acceptanceCriteria: Search form accepts blood group selection and city text input
Results list shows only donors matching the selected blood group and city
Results are sorted by most recently active first
Donors active within 24 hours display a green 'Available Now' badge
Each result card shows donor name, blood group, city, and phone number

requirements.text: Add a prominent red SOS Emergency Request button on the home screen. When pressed, it creates an urgent blood request record in the backend (with the requester's blood group, city, and timestamp) and displays a confirmation message that the request has been broadcast to nearby matching donors.

quotes: Big red SOS button on home screen

acceptanceCriteria: Home screen displays a large, visually distinct red SOS button
Pressing the button prompts confirmation before submitting
On confirmation, an emergency request record is created in the backend with blood group, city, and timestamp
A success message confirms the request was sent
Emergency requests are queryable separately from normal requests in the backend

requirements.text: Build an AI Eligibility Checker feature as a step-by-step questionnaire. Ask: age, weight (kg), date of last donation, and current health conditions (checkboxes: fever, recent surgery, pregnancy, medication, none). Based on the answers, display either 'Eligible to Donate' or 'Not Eligible' with a reason, and if eligible, show the next recommended donation date (minimum 3 months after last donation).

quotes: Ask simple questions: Age, Weight, Last donation, Health conditions

acceptanceCriteria: Questionnaire is presented one step at a time with progress indication
Age under 18 or over 65 results in 'Not Eligible'
Weight under 50 kg results in 'Not Eligible'
Any disqualifying health condition (fever, recent surgery, pregnancy, active medication) results in 'Not Eligible' with the specific reason shown
If last donation was less than 3 months ago, user is shown the next eligible donation date
Eligible users see a green 'Eligible to Donate' result with the next donation date

requirements.text: On each donor result card in the search results, add a one-tap 'Contact via WhatsApp' button that opens a pre-filled WhatsApp message to the donor's phone number using the wa.me deep link (https://wa.me/<phone_number>?text=...) with a default message: 'Hi, I found your profile on Blood Connect. I urgently need blood. Can you help?'
acceptanceCriteria: Each donor card in search results has a WhatsApp button
Tapping the button opens WhatsApp with the donor's number and a pre-filled message
The link uses the wa.me format with the donor's stored phone number
Button is visually styled in WhatsApp green

requirements.text: Build a Dashboard screen showing: total registered donors count, a list of donors in the current user's city (nearby donors), and the 5 most recent emergency requests (blood group, city, time ago). Fetch all counts and lists from the backend.
acceptanceCriteria: Dashboard displays total donor count as a prominent stat
Nearby donors section lists donors matching the logged-in user's city
Recent requests section shows the 5 latest emergency requests with blood group, city, and relative time
All data is fetched from backend queries
Dashboard refreshes data on each visit

requirements.text: Build an Admin Panel accessible via a separate route (/admin). It should display: a paginated list of all registered users (name, role, blood group, city, phone), a button to remove/deactivate any user (mark as removed in backend, do not delete), and a list of all emergency requests with requester name, blood group, city, and timestamp.
acceptanceCriteria: Admin panel is accessible at /admin route
All users are listed with name, role, blood group, city, and phone
Each user row has a 'Remove' button that deactivates the user in the backend
Removed users are hidden from donor search results
All emergency requests are listed with requester info and timestamp
Admin panel has no authentication gate in this initial version (access by URL)

requirements.text: Apply a clean medical design theme throughout the app: white backgrounds, red (#DC2626) as the primary accent color, large readable fonts (minimum 16px body text), large touch targets (minimum 48px tall buttons), and a simple card-based layout. The app should feel usable on small screens (mobile-first, max 430px wide layout). Include English as the display language for all UI text.

quotes: Make the app fast, lightweight, and usable even on low-end Android phones

acceptanceCriteria: Primary accent color is red (#DC2626) used on buttons, badges, and highlights
All background surfaces are white or light gray
Body text is at minimum 16px; headings are at minimum 20px
All interactive buttons have a minimum height of 48px
Layout is centered and constrained to mobile width (max ~430px)
SOS button is visually dominant on the home screen
No blue or purple in the color palette

requirements.text: Use a generated Blood Connect logo image as the app header logo on the home screen and navigation bar.
acceptanceCriteria: Logo image is displayed in the app header
Logo is loaded from frontend/public/assets/generated/
Logo is appropriately sized for a mobile header (height ~40px)

constraints: Do not integrate real-time WebSockets or push notification services — simulate notification confirmation with UI feedback only
Do not integrate external AI/LLM APIs — implement eligibility checker logic as rule-based frontend logic
Do not integrate external map/GPS services — use city name text for location matching
All backend state must be stored in the single Motoko actor
Do not add authentication beyond Internet Identity; admin panel access is unguarded in this version
nonGoals: Real-time push notifications to mobile devices
SMS notifications via Twilio or any external SMS service
Tamil language localization (UI will be in English only)
In-app chat system (WhatsApp deep link is used instead)
Native Android/iOS app packaging — this is a web app only

required: Blood Connect app logo — a clean medical-style icon with a red heart containing a blood drop, white background, minimal flat design (blood-connect-logo.dim_256x256.png)
Red SOS emergency button graphic — bold red circular button with white 'SOS' text and pulse ring effect (sos-button.dim_256x256.png)
